channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Curry scores 37 points and Warriors beat Lakers 123-116 in a potential first-round playoff preview
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Stephen Curry scored 37 points, Brandin Podziemski added 28 and the Golden State Warriors defeated the Los Angeles Lakers 123-116 on Thursday night for their fourth win in a row in a potential first-round playoff preview.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Reds have lost 1-0 in each of their last three games. No team had done that since 1960
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — The Cincinnati Reds’ re-creation of the dead-ball era has them doing something that hadn’t happened in the major leagues in 65 years. Cincinnati has lost 1-0 in each of its last three games.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Blues edge Penguins 5-4 in OT, running win streak to franchise-tying 11 games
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — Robert Thomas scored a power-play goal in overtime and the St. Louis Blues tied a franchise record with their 11th consecutive win, 5-4 over the Pittsburgh Penguins on Thursday night. Jake Neighbours scored twice and Pavel Buchnevich and Jordan Kyrou also scored for the Blues.
item.dc:creator: BY JOE HARRIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Morant beats the buzzer for a game-winner, lifts Grizzlies past Heat 110-108
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Ja Morant made a 12-foot jumper in the lane as time expired, lifting the Memphis Grizzlies to a 110-108 win over the Miami Heat on Thursday night and giving interim coach Tuomas Iisalo his first victory with the team.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Giannis' NBA 1st of 35 points, 20 assists, 17 rebounds sends Bucks past 76ers 126-113
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Giannis Antetokounmpo became the first player in NBA history to have 35 points, 17 rebounds and a career-high 20 assists as the Milwaukee Bucks beat the Philadelphia 76ers 126-113 Thursday night. Antetokounmpo helped the Bucks shoot 51.6% from 3-point range.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dodgers place Freddie Freeman on IL after 'freak accident' in the shower
item.description: Freddie Freeman found himself on the injured list this week, days after he slipped and fell in the shower at home and reinjured the ankle he hurt in the postseason.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who is Stephanie Turner? Women's fencer who knelt to protest trans opponent and ignited global awareness
item.description: Fencer Stephanie Turner was a Democrat with LGBT friends, but she decided to kneel in forfeit rather than face a transgender opponent.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tulane suspends new quarterback TJ Finley after his arrest
item.description: T.J. Finley, who recently transferred to his fifth school in Tulane, was arrested this week for allegedly possessing a stolen vehicle and has been suspended from the team.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sam Ryder takes only 21 putts for a 63 and leads Texas Open
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Sam Ryder wasn't sure his neck would feel good enough for him to play Thursday in the Valero Texas Open. He found a swing and hardly missed a putt on his way to a 9-under 63 and a one-shot lead over Keith Mitchell in the final tournament before the Masters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Family of Bucknell football player who died after collapsing during first practice files lawsuit
item.description: The family of Calvin Dickey Jr. has filed a lawsuit against Bucknell University claiming negligence in the death of their son, who died after collapsing during a team practice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chicagoland foursome heads to Augusta for Drive, Chip and Putt Finals
item.description: GOLF, Ill. - Mastering golf can take a lifetime. Luckily, these four started early. "When I was like 2, I was holding a plastic club, watching a golf tournament - I don't remember which one," remembered 12-year-old Winnetka native Jack Kemper.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: Braves are desperate for recovery as they enter their home opener as the majors' only winless team
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The Atlanta Braves' longest winless start since 2016 has left manager Brian Snitker desperate for a recovery he hopes begins with the team's home opener against Miami on Friday night. Atlanta blew a 5-0 lead and lost 6-5 on Shohei Ohtani's ninth-inning homer on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Saquon Barkley shouts out Ivanka Trump, Kushner brothers after having 'amazing time' at summit
item.description: Eagles running back Saquon Barkley shouted out Ivanka Trump ahead of his visit to the White House to celebrate his team's Super Bowl title.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tight end Trey McBride agrees to 4-year deal with Cardinals, keeping him with team through 2029
item.description: Star tight end Trey McBride has agreed to a four-year deal with the Arizona Cardinals as the franchise locks up one of the NFL's best young players through the 2029 season. The Cardinals confirmed the signing Thursday.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fencer refuses to compete against transgender opponent
item.description: Turner told NewsNation she didn’t “want to let people know that I'm OK with this and pretend like nothing's happening.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: USC star JuJu Watkins is the AP Player of the Year and just the fourth sophomore to earn that honor
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — JuJu Watkins, the sensational sophomore who led Southern California to its best season in nearly 40 years, was honored Thursday as The Associated Press women’s basketball Player of the Year.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Auburn's Broome says there's no pain in his elbow and he will be ready to face Florida in Final Four
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Auburn forward Johni Broome returned to practice for the Tigers on Thursday and said he has no pain in his right elbow and “no limitations” ahead of the Tigers' Final Four matchup with Florida.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bodybuilder who starred in Netflix show dead at 44 after having heart attack on treadmill: report
item.description: Vittorio “Vito" Pirbazari, a 44-year-old bodybuilder from Germany who appeared on the Netflix show "Dogs of Berlin," died after training on a treadmill.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bay FC star removed from Zambian national team due to ‘additional travel measures’ under Trump administration
item.description: Bay FC star Racheal Kundananji and three Orlando Pride players have been removed from the Zambia national team for upcoming international matches due to added challenges entering the United States.
item.dc:creator: Staff and wire reports, The Mercury News

item.title: FIFA president Infantino: US to host 2031 Women’s World Cup; CONCACAF may join project
item.description: “We look forward to sharing more details and unveiling our full vision for the 2031 FIFA Women’s World Cup in the near future,” the U.S. Soccer Federation said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: SafeSport knew of allegations against former cop before hiring him as investigator
item.description: People at the U.S. Center for SafeSport knew a former police officer was the subject of an internal investigation at his former job but hired him anyway, according to details released Wednesday by Sen. Chuck Grassley, who is looking into the matter.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Miller Gardner, son of ex-Yankees star, died from carbon monoxide poisoning, officials say
item.description: Officials in Costa Rica confirmed Wednesday that the official cause of death for 14-year-old Miller Gardner was carbon monoxide poisoning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US to host Military World Games for the first time ever
item.description: The U.S. will host the Military World Games for the first time in 2027, when it comes to Charlotte, North Carolina, bringing soldiers from across the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jon Scheyer, a Chicagoland prep basketball legend, going for 3rd national title with Duke
item.description: Jon Scheyer is two victories shy of winning his third NCAA Division I national championship with the storied Duke men's basketball program: one as a player, one as an assistant coach, and — with wins in San Antonio on Saturday and Monday — one as a head coach.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: White Sox fall to Twins, 6-1, to drop rubber match of 3-game series after lengthy rain delay on South Side
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Harrison Bader and Byron Buxton hit home runs to back Pablo López's solid start, and the Minnesota Twins beat the Chicago White Sox, 6-1, on Wednesday after rain delayed the start by nearly 3 1/2 hours.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 2025 NASCAR Chicago Street Race: City, NASCAR announce traffic plan for before, during, after race weekend
item.description: CHICAGO — The City of Chicago and NASCAR officials have announced the traffic plan for the third NASCAR Chicago Street Race, scheduled for July 5-6 (Saturday and Sunday) in Grant Park.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9
